findings.evidence: `Subcomodule` is defined over `[CommSemiring R] [AddCommMonoid C]` in `Subcomodule.lean:41-45`; Mathlib `Module.Flat.rTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap` is under `[CommSemiring R] [AddCommMonoid M]` in `RingTheory/Flat/Basic.lean:84-120`, and the diff never uses subtraction in `C`.
findings.fix: Weaken the carrier assumption to `[AddCommMonoid C]`; keep additive group assumptions only where quotient/exact-subtype facts force them for `M`, `N`, and later targets.
findings.issue: `comap` requires `[AddCommGroup C]`, but the proof only tensors with `C` and uses flatness/right-exactness APIs that are stated for additive commutative monoids; this unnecessarily excludes coalgebra carriers supported by the existing `Subcomodule` API.

findings.evidence: Mathlib’s corresponding submodule theorem is `le_comap_map (f) (p) : p ≤ comap f (map f p)` in `Algebra/Module/Submodule/Map.lean:265-267`; the PR’s theorem at `Comap.lean:169-172` requires `{A D}` and `hAD : A ≤ D`.
findings.fix: State `theorem le_comap_map (A : Subcomodule R C M) (f : Comodule.Hom R C M N) : A ≤ (A.map f).comap f` and, if needed, add the current stronger variant under a distinct name such as `le_comap_map_of_le`.
findings.issue: `le_comap_map` is named like the standard unit of the map/comap Galois connection but is stated with an extra larger subcomodule `D` and hypothesis `A ≤ D`, so the natural theorem `A ≤ (A.map f).comap f` is not available under the expected signature.

summary: The comap construction is mostly at the right level, but the exposed assumptions and one standard API theorem are not. Fix the overstrong carrier assumption and restore the usual `le_comap_map` shape.
